codeReview.summary: The fix closes the blocking retry-path bug from the original migration review by separating an empty first migration check from a finalized legacy merge, and it preserves the no-resurface semantics after canonical deletes with focused regression coverage.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The canonical watchlist migration now relies on an additional persisted invariant, `legacy_migration_completed_with_favorites`, to distinguish a finalized merge that consumed visible legacy data from an empty canonical document that must still retry. Shared architecture/contracts docs still describe canonical ownership and identifier rules but do not document this migration-state field or how `legacy_migration_checked_at`, `legacy_migrated_at`, and `legacy_migration_completed_with_favorites` interact.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: app/services/favorites_service.py:172-236 and 239-317 persist/read `legacy_migration_checked_at`, `legacy_migrated_at`, and `legacy_migration_completed_with_favorites`; .factory/library/architecture.md and .factory/library/contracts.md contain no migration-marker guidance.

summary: Reviewed the prior failing migration commit `c42f32a2`, the fix commit `a492527e`, the fix worker transcript skeleton, handoffs, and shared-state docs. The original blocking issue was that an empty first canonical touch could finalize migration and permanently hide later-visible legacy favorites. The fix now keeps that path retry-safe until legacy data is truly observed and merged, while preserving the original requirement that already-consumed legacy rows do not reappear after canonical delete/read flows. Focused and repo-wide pytest evidence in the handoff supports the behavior, and the updated tests cover both delayed visibility and post-merge delete scenarios.
